stdout:
I’m checking the current diff, prior review findings, and validation signals together so I can verify whether the remaining blockers were actually fixed.

I confirmed the prior blockers appear fixed. I’m spot-checking the actual code and docs surfaces now so the verdict is based on the current tree, not just the earlier review history.

The earlier `--json` and PID-safety blockers are fixed. I’m checking whether any subtler contract gaps remain, especially around docs, `--fg`, and test coverage.

I found `jit serve` is already documented in the CLI reference, so the earlier “missing docs” note no longer holds. I’m checking the command definition and test surfaces to see whether anything else is still review-blocking.

I don’t see any dedicated CLI or harness tests for `jit serve` yet, so I’m confirming whether the feature is only unit-tested. If so, that’s likely the biggest remaining review concern.

I’ve verified there are still no dedicated integration or harness tests exercising `jit serve` through the CLI. I’m doing one last pass on docs/API requirements before I write the review.

## Acceptance criteria

Most acceptance criteria are implemented: detached start/stop/status are wired in `crates/jit/src/main.rs:3521-3690`, PID-file lifecycle lives in `crates/jit/src/commands/serve.rs:287-425`, `jit-server --web-dir` is present in `crates/server/src/main.rs:37-42,92-95`, and `serve` is hidden from default MCP listing in `crates/jit/src/schema.rs:144-193`.

I do not consider the review passable yet because two parts of the exposed command interface are still incorrect: `--log` reports the wrong file path, and `--fg` reports a fake PID after the server has already exited.

## Architecture and separation of concerns

No blocking boundary violation found. Process lifecycle logic stays in `crates/jit/src/commands/serve.rs`, CLI/output handling stays in `crates/jit/src/main.rs`, and static file serving remains in `crates/server/src/main.rs`.

## Functional paradigm

No blocking issue here. The port selection logic in `crates/jit/src/commands/serve.rs:184-193` uses iterator combinators cleanly.

## Correctness and invariants

Blocking: `jit serve --log FILE` produces incorrect output. The user-supplied path is parsed at `crates/jit/src/main.rs:3536`, but start success output hard-codes `jit_dir.join("server.log")` at `3643-3653`. Plain output and JSON both therefore lie about the actual log path when `--log` is used.

Blocking: foreground mode is semantically wrong. `crates/jit/src/commands/serve.rs:329-336` calls `cmd.status()`, which waits for `jit-server` to exit, then returns `ServeOutcome::Started { pid: 0, port }`. `crates/jit/src/main.rs:3641-3659` then prints `Server started ... (PID 0)`. That is not a live server start report; it is a post-exit message with impossible runtime metadata.

PID safety itself is fixed: `crates/jit/src/commands/serve.rs:393-399` now rejects `pid_i32 <= 0`.

Atomic PID writes are correct at `crates/jit/src/commands/serve.rs:120-127`.

## Safety

No `unsafe` usage observed. The earlier PID signaling hazard appears addressed.

## Testing

Blocking: coverage does not match the feature surface. The only dedicated tests I found are unit tests in `crates/jit/src/commands/serve.rs:431-628`, mostly for helper functions and stale PID cleanup. I found no harness/integration tests under `crates/jit/tests/` exercising the CLI behavior in `crates/jit/src/main.rs:3538-3690` for `--json`, `--stop`, `--status`, idempotent re-run, hidden-schema exposure, `--log`, or `--fg`. For a new CLI command with output contracts, that is insufficient per the repo’s TDD/testing standard.

## Error handling

Prior review feedback on JSON error handling is addressed. `crates/jit/src/main.rs:3564-3576`, `3612-3624`, and `3678-3690` now emit structured JSON errors when `--json` is set.

## CLI contract

Blocking: the machine-readable contract is still wrong for successful start output when `--log` is used. `crates/jit/src/main.rs:3647-3653` returns a hard-coded `log_file` value instead of the effective path actually passed into `ServeOptions`.

## Documentation

Blocking: many new public APIs in `crates/jit/src/commands/serve.rs` still do not meet the project rustdoc standard requiring `# Examples`. Examples exist for `ServerPidFile`, `ServeOutcome`, `StopOutcome`, and `find_web_dir` (`20-36`, `51-70`, `79-91`, `242-258`), but are still missing for `pid_file_path` (`104-105`), `read_pid_file` (`109-117`), `write_pid_file` (`120-127`), `remove_pid_file` (`130-137`), `is_process_alive` (`144-154`), `find_available_port` (`181-184`), `find_server_binary` (`199-204`), `ServeOptions` (`226-240`), `start_server` (`285-287`), `stop_server` (`374-375`), and `server_status` (`414-415`).

## Prior review feedback

The most recent failed review’s blockers were addressed: JSON error paths are fixed, PID `0` signaling is guarded, and some rustdoc examples were added.

However, the change still does not pass review because the current tree introduces/retains the blocking issues above.

## Jit issue management

`issue.dependencies` is empty, so there are no prerequisite issues to verify.

I could not independently rerun `jit validate` or cargo commands in this environment because command execution was denied by the runtime, although the provided gate state shows `tests`, `clippy`, and `fmt` passed. The code findings above are sufficient to fail the review regardless.

VERDICT: FAIL
---
Gate result: FAILED
